spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates reaching into document.activeViewTransition from a separate module to call skipTransition() and attach observers to ready, finished, and updateCallbackDone — exactly the motivation the spec gives.

interactive.rationale: Five real controls (swap A, swap B, external skip, prefers-reduced-motion auto-skip, clear log), a live observer grid for the three promises, and a progress bar tied to actual transition timing.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index sells 'a back-button handler, reduced-motion watcher, telemetry layer reaches into the live transition' and the page enumerates each of those use cases with a working button for each.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers external skip mid-flight and the reduced-motion auto-skip pattern, plus the rejected-promise paths on ready/finished. Missing: what happens when skipTransition is called BEFORE ready resolves (the spec's spec-edge case), and the popstate path mentioned in the 'why this is useful' list isn't actually demoed.

references.rationale: Links chromestatus.com/feature/5067126381215744 and the css-view-transitions-2 anchor for the property.

design_system.rationale: Colours via CSS variables, log uses --accent-emerald/amber/rose channels, no static-card pattern. Uses color-mix() with --bg-paper as the base which keeps WCAG AA contrast.

openQuestions.title: Wire up the popstate handler claim
openQuestions.detail: The 'why this is useful' list highlights back-button handling as the canonical use case but the page never actually fires history.back() / pushState. Add a 'simulate browser back' button that fires popstate and shows the external module catching the in-flight transition the same way it catches a manual click.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate skipTransition() before ready resolves
openQuestions.detail: The spec splits the transition into pending-capture, update-callback-called, animating, done phases. Calling skipTransition during pending-capture vs animating behaves differently (the former rejects ready with AbortError before it ever fulfills). A 'skip immediately' button that calls skip in the same task as startViewTransition would surface that path.

openQuestions.title: Show real matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') wiring
openQuestions.detail: The current toggle is a manual button labelled 'prefers reduced motion auto-skip' but it's not actually wired to matchMedia. Replace with a real listener that flips on OS-level reduced-motion change events so the page demonstrates the integration pattern, not just a hand-rolled flag.

summary: Excellent multi-module demo — three real consumer use cases on one page (skip, observe, reduced-motion), with live promise readouts. Two improvements would round it out: a real popstate-fed external skip and an actual matchMedia listener for the reduced-motion path.
